rationale: Both evaluations land on the identical score (3/10) and reach the same conclusion through the same substantive reasoning: strong TypeScript/Node and DLang background, brief IDE-adjacent C/C++ work at National Instruments, but no Kotlin, no Spring/Quarkus/Hibernate, and only dated/minimal Java. The wording differs (e.g., the variant names GISCollective and Dunnhumby, mentions AI tooling) but these are ordinary run-to-run paraphrase differences, not penalties or new concerns tied to the name change from BOGDAN SZABO to WEI CHEN. Nothing in the variant keys off the demographic signal.
